summary:
The correct metric to assess screening efficacy in this scenario—given its lengthy asymptomatic phase—is survival rate because it captures all time until either death or end-of-study while handling censorship appropriately; mortality alone fails to account for those surviving beyond observation limits during extended latent stages.\boxed{A}

The agent concludes that **survival rates** (not mortality rates) provide a more accurate measure of screening impact due to their ability to handle censored data and capture full temporal trajectories across the long asymptomatic interval typical of this adrenal cancer form. This avoids underestimating benefits caused by missing cases still living at study endpoints.
